name: Tomato-Stuffed Eggplant
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
2. Slice eggplants in half lengthwise. Remove pulp, leaving about a 1/2 inch layer on the outer shell.
3. Chop 1 cup of the pulp.
4. In a skillet, sauté onion and garlic in oil along with tomato wedges and chopped eggplant until all vegetables are soft.
5. Mix in chives, bread crumbs, dried crushed basil, oregano, thyme, salt (or substitute), pepper (freshly cracked), and 2-1/2 cups of tomato juice.
6. Stuff shells and arrange them in an oven-proof dish.
7. Pour the remaining tomato juice into the bottom of the pan.
8. Bake for about 30 minutes, basting with juice to keep moist.
9. Garnish with parsley.
